summary: Pre-tournament favourites and reigning European champions, chasing the Euro+World Cup double under Luis de la Fuente. Flawless lately — 21 goals and just 2 conceded in qualifying, scoring at three a game since 2023, with one 'loss' in three years (a Nations League final shootout to Portugal). A well-defined, all-controlling possession side.
style: Fluid 4-3-3 / 4-2-3-1 — build with a three-man defence plus a deep midfielder (Zubimendi), two midfielders pushing on and full-backs joining to leave five or six across the opposition line. Constant rotations and incisive, penetrative passing through midfield. No classic No 9 (Ferran Torres, Oyarzabal drift in and out); midfielders arrive late onto cut-backs and crosses.

counterReliance.note: All-controlling possession; counter-press rather than counter

strengths: Dominant, purposeful possession with synchronised movement that prises defences apart
Deep squad steeped in the style — 15 of the 26 played U21 under De la Fuente; Rodri, Pedri, Fabián in midfield
Elite counter-press and rest-defence positioning — most final-third ball recoveries at Euro 2024
weaknesses: Brave, risky rest-defence (two CBs while both full-backs push on) — exposed if the ball is lost
Best route to hurt them: win physical duels high up and counter the exposed defence

keyPlayers.name: Lamine Yamal
keyPlayers.note: At 18, the world's best dribbler — unpredictable, slaloms through challenges and opens space for others; a hamstring issue may limit his early group games

keyPlayers.name: Pedri
keyPlayers.note: The metronome of the positional play, dictating tempo through the middle

tactics: Control the game, keep five across the front and rotate to unlock space, then smother any loss with the counter-press. Cede them the ball and the passing and pressing will suffocate you; the only real opening is their committed rest-defence — if you can win possession first.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group H (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
